title: CORS allows all origins with credentials — cross-origin requests can carry cookies/auth headers from any site
exploit: Attacker hosts a malicious page that fires authenticated cross-origin requests to this API; browser attaches cookies; API accepts them because origin is unrestricted and credentials:true
fix: Replace origin: '*' with an explicit allowlist; never combine wildcard origin with credentials: true

title: No Content-Security-Policy header — inline scripts and arbitrary external sources permitted
exploit: XSS payload executes without restriction; attacker can inject arbitrary scripts, phone home exfiltrating cookies/tokens, or redefine page behaviour
fix: Add a strict CSP via helmet.contentSecurityPolicy(); set default-src 'none', allow only known origins for script-src
evidence: // TODO: add helmet() for CSP and X-Frame-Options  // no CSP set

title: Session cookie set without HttpOnly or Secure flags — accessible to JS and transmitted over HTTP
exploit: httpOnly: false means XSS can read the session token via document.cookie; secure: false means it transmits over plain HTTP and can be stolen by a network observer
fix: Set httpOnly: true, secure: true (enforce HTTPS), and add sameSite: 'Strict' or 'Lax'

title: Third-party script loaded without Subresource Integrity — CDN compromise delivers arbitrary code
exploit: If CDN is compromised or the script tag is rewritten by a network attacker, the browser executes whatever the CDN returns — no integrity check prevents it
fix: Add integrity="sha384-<hash>" and crossorigin="anonymous" to every external script/style tag

title: postMessage handler sets innerHTML from any origin — DOM XSS from any window
exploit: Any iframe or popup on any origin can post an arbitrary HTML payload; the handler sets innerHTML without checking event.origin, executing injected scripts
fix: Check event.origin against a known-good allowlist before acting on the message; sanitize or avoid innerHTML

title: No X-Frame-Options or frame-ancestors CSP directive — page embeddable in any iframe (clickjacking)
exploit: Attacker overlays a transparent iframe of this page over a decoy button; victim's clicks are hijacked to trigger authenticated actions (e.g., delete account, transfer funds)
fix: Add X-Frame-Options: DENY or a CSP frame-ancestors 'none' directive via helmet.frameguard()
evidence: // TODO: add helmet() for CSP and X-Frame-Options  // no frameguard set
